title: sauvetrea grandimentum
side_effects: Sauvetrea grandimentum can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe stomach cramps, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high toxicity levels. Prolonged exposure to its alkaloids can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue in individuals who are not familiar with its usage. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Sauvetrea grandimentum can cause kidney damage, liver failure, and respiratory distress. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using this plant for any health-related purposes to avoid these adverse effects.
